titles.text: アンドレ・アガシ テニス

descriptions.text: アンドレ・アガシ テニスは、90年代初頭に「イメージが全て」というスタイルでテニス界を驚かせたアメリカの選手、アンドレ・アガシが公認したゲームです。長髪、イヤリング、カラフルなシャツを身にまとったアガシを含む8人のプレイヤー（男性と女性）が登場し、動きの速さ、サーブ、バックハンド、フォアハンドの精度と力に基づいて評価されています。ゲームプレイにはスマッシュ、パッシングショット、ボレー、さまざまなバックハンドとフォアハンドのプレイが含まれますが、他のゲームとは異なり、正確なポジショニングとタイミングが重要です。ボールをコートの外に打ってしまったり、ラケットを空振りしたり、さらにはボールが頭に当たるという恥ずかしい事態を避けるためです。3つのコートが用意されています。ゲームモードはバージョンによって異なり、16ビット版には「スキンズ」ゲームが含まれており、各ポイントはボールが打たれた回数に基づいて金額が設定されています。

A game endorsed by the American player who stunned the tennis world in the early 90s with his "Image is everything" looks, sporting long hair, earrings and colorful shirts, Andre Agassi Tennis includes eight players (male and female and as as expected, only Agassi is a real player) rated according to movement speed and both accuracy and strength on serve, backhand and forehand.

Gameplay features all the usual moves: smashes, passing shots, volleys and all kinds of backhand and forehand plays, but unlike other games, precise positioning and timing are crucial to avoid hitting the ball outside the court, swinging the racket into thin air or more embarrassing, let the ball hit you on the head.
Three courts are available: Grass, Clay and Indoor (Sega versions add a fourth, Hard), but the differences between them are minimal. Game modes change according to the version, with the 16-bit versions including a "Skins" game, where each point is worth a sum of money based on the number of times the ball was hit.

The player can play in either career mode, exhibition mode, or practice mode where the player can learn to hit and receive tennis balls in a proper manner in order to assure victory on the tennis court.

During the career mode, the player travels to tennis courts around the world and compete in tournaments where victory allows to play to win money. After mastering all the tournaments, the player actually plays against Mr. Andre Agassi in a final grudge match in order to determine who is the best tennis player in the world. All the other players are generic except for him.
